- `[Summary item]`

## Acceptance Criteria

Copy the ticket acceptance criteria and mark each item when satisfied.

- [ ] `[Acceptance criterion]`
- [ ] `[Acceptance criterion]`

## Source of Truth

Confirm the work follows:

- [ ] [PROJECT_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md](../PROJECT_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md)
- [ ] [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md)
- [ ] [CONTENT_GUARDRAILS.md](../CONTENT_GUARDRAILS.md)
- [ ] [STYLE_GUIDE.md](../STYLE_GUIDE.md)
- [ ] [Definition of Done](../docs/start-here/definition-of-done.md)

## Scope and Originality

- [ ] The diff is limited to the ticket deliverable path and necessary navigation or index updates.
- [ ] The content is original and does not copy or closely paraphrase books, courses, articles, diagrams, tables, screenshots, or exercises.
- [ ] Examples, prompts, and diagrams are invented for this project.
- [ ] External sources are cited only for specific factual claims when needed.
- [ ] No logos, screenshots, publisher branding, or proprietary content were added without explicit permission.

## Style, Links, and Navigation

- [ ] Writing follows the project style guide: direct, practical, and decision-focused.
- [ ] Related pages are linked where useful.
- [ ] New docs pages are linked from section indexes or existing navigation when those files exist.
- [ ] Markdown links point to existing local targets or intentionally external references.
- [ ] Tables, checklists, and examples are readable in Markdown.

## Diagrams

Complete this section only when diagrams changed.

- [ ] Mermaid diagrams are original and clarify a decision, flow, state transition, architecture relationship, or failure mode.
- [ ] Diagram labels are readable and specific.
- [ ] Queues, stores, trust boundaries, or async paths are shown when they matter.
- [ ] Not applicable: no diagrams changed.

## Labs

Complete this section only when labs changed.

- [ ] Lab README, design, run instructions, expected output, what-to-observe, trade-offs, implementation, and tests or demo script are included when this PR changes a lab.
- [ ] Python and pytest defaults are followed unless the ticket states otherwise.
- [ ] Not applicable: no labs changed.

## Checks Run

List commands and outcomes.

```text
[command] - [passed/failed/not available]
```

## Review Readiness

- [ ] The acceptance criteria are satisfied.
- [ ] Dependencies were checked.
- [ ] The PR makes clear what decision, trade-off, failure mode, or practice skill it helps readers understand.
- [ ] Trade-offs and failure modes are explicit where relevant.
- [ ] Security, observability, cost, and simplification are covered where relevant.
- [ ] Missing checks or unavailable tooling are noted.
- [ ] The PR is small enough to review against the ticket scope.
